title: ISic000630. Honours for L. Cassius Manilianus
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/9KMBP7J8
1L(ucio) Cassio Manili-
2ano c(larissimo) v(iro) curioni
3minori q(uaestori) p(rovinciae) S(iciliae) curat(ori)
4rei p(ublicae) Lilyb(itanorum) ob insigneṃ
5eius abstinentiam pa-
6tientiam praestante-
7[a]m erga patriam su[am]
8ordo splend[idi]ṣs(imus) col(oniae) Lily(bitanorum)
9decreto pubḷ[ic]ọ facto
10[s]ṭatiam in patria eius
11p(ecunia) p(ublica) posuit

l.10 reg., orig. statiam

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/9KMBP7J8
1LCASSIOMANILI
2ANOCVCURIONI
3MINORIQPSCURAT
4REIPLILYBOBINSIGNE.
5EIUSABSTINENTIAMPA
6TIENTIAMPRAESTANTE
7[.]MERGAPATRIAMSU[..]
8ORDOSPLEND[...].SCOLLILY
9DECRETOPUB.[..].FACTO
10[.].ATIAMINPATRIAEIUS
11PPPOSUIT

l.10 reg., orig. statiam

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Bivona, controlled against photographs;
  • 4: Bivona: insigne(m); Jacques: insignem
  • 6: Bivona, Jacques: praestanti; lapis; praestante
  • 8: Jacques: splend[idu]s
  • 9: Jacques: decre(vit) popu(li) [suffr]agio
  • 10: Bivona: ẹṭ aliam; Jacques: [e]t aliam

English translation

Jonathan Prag

For Lucius Cassius Manilianus, vir clarissimus, curio minor, quaestor of the province of Sicily, curator of the res publica of the Lilybitani, in recognition of his remarkable self-control, patience and pre-eminence shown towards his fatherland, the most illustrious senate of the colonia of the Lilybitani, by public decree, set up a statue at public expense in his fatherland.

commentary

Cassius might be the same man as recorded in an inscription from Furnos in Africa Prconsularis (CIL VIII.23801), of the same period (so Bivona). He is one of several curatores of Lilybaeum known in the third century (the inscription dates to this period palaeographically and on the basis of the cursus described). Jacques and Bivona published their editions contemporaneously but independently of one another; the former working only from the photograph published by Manganaro. In general Bivona's readings are to be preferred, but Wilson's proposed reading of line 10 (statiam for statuam) is both more logical and also aligns with the traces on the stone, which suggest T rather than L.

bibliography

  • 1990.0438 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • Napoli 1923 155-159 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001731
  • Bivona 1987 11-23 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001487
  • Manganaro 1988 53 tav.XXII.34 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001427
  • Wilson 1990 326 n.36 fig.278 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001509
  • Jacques 1990 163-170 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001487

Tall narrow letters, but the first three lines show a wider module and straighter, more 'monumental' style.

Line 1-11 60 Interlineation line 1 to 2
Licensed under a Creative Commons-Attribution 4.0 licence.
